steps.title: State the condition for infinitely many solutions
steps.content: An equation in $x$ has infinitely many solutions when both sides are identical expressions for all $x$. Factor the left side: $$4x + 12 = 4(x + 3)$$ So the equation becomes: $$4(x+3) = \frac{a(x+3)}{2}$$

steps.title: Solve for a
steps.content: Multiply both sides by $2$: $$8(x+3)=a(x+3)$$ For this to hold for every $x$, the coefficient of $(x+3)$ must match, so $a=8$. A Desmos regression using two simple $x$-values also returns $a=8$.

steps.title: Test all four choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — '0': $a=0$ gives $4x+12=0$, true only when $x=-3$ — exactly one solution, not infinitely many. ✗
  • Choice B — '3': $a=3$ gives $4x+12 = \frac{3}{2}x + \frac{9}{2}$. Coefficients $4 \neq \frac{3}{2}$, so exactly one solution. ✗
  • Choice C — '8': $a=8$ gives right side $\frac{8(x+3)}{2} = 4(x+3) = 4x+12$. Both sides identical — infinitely many solutions. ✓
  • Choice D — '12': $a=12$ gives right side $\frac{12(x+3)}{2} = 6x+18$. Coefficients $4 \neq 6$, so exactly one solution. ✗
That matches choice C — C is correct.
